properties.remark: REPORTS RANGING FROM QUARTER TO GOLFBALL SIZE HAIL IN TYE.
QUARTER HAIL COVERING THE GROUND JUST NORTH OF THE KTXS NEWS STATION ON HIGHWAY 277.
NEAR HWY 89.
GOLF BALL HAIL ON HWY 84
1 INCH HAIL REPORTED AT INTERSECTION OF RIDGEMONT AND REBECCA LANES
1 INCH HAIL REPORTED AT INTERSECTION OF RIDGEMONT AND REBECCA LANES
A PICTURE WAS TAKEN OF THIS TORNADO AS IT WAS REPORTED BY THE PUBLIC WITH NO DAMAGE REPORTED.
SIX POWER POLES WERE REPORTED DOWN AT REBECCA LANE AND 277 ON THE WEST SIDE OF ABILENE. THE POLES WERE BLOWN OVER FROM WEST TO EAST.
POWER LINES WERE BLOWN DOWN.
QUARTER SIZE HAIL WAS REPORTED AT THE INTERSECTION OF U.S HWY 277 AND REECE ROAD.
A CARPORT WAS BLOWN OVER ONTO SOME CARS 6 MILES NORTHEAST OF JUNCTION ON US HWY 377.
QUARTER SIZE HAIL REPORTED 6 MILES NORTHEAST OF JUNCTION ON US HWY 377.
REPORT OF A BRIEF TORNADO TOUCHDOWN ON I-20 WEST OF TYE
REPORT OF A BRIEF TORNADO TOUCHDOWN 2 MILES NORTHEAST OF TYE...ABOUT 3/4 MI NORTH OF I-20.
BASEBALL HAIL AT THE INTERSECTION OF HWYS. 351 AND 604.

THE NWS CONDUCTED ANOTHER SURVEY EARLIER TODAY. IT WAS FOUND THAT A TORNADO TORE THE ROOF AND SOME WALLS OFF OF THE UPPER FLOOR OF A RESIDENTIAL HOME. THE TORNADO WAS ON THE GROUND FOR 1.5 MILES AND WAS ABOUT 75 YARDS WIDE. THE TORNADO WAS RATED AS EF0.
A LARGE TREE WAS BLOWN OVER ALONG HIGHWAY 153.

NWS STORM SURVEY REVEALED THAT AN EF0 TORNADO SNAPPED TREE LIMBS NEAR THE TWIN BUTTES EQUALIZATION CHANNEL AND DOWNED 6 UTILITY POLES ALONG SCHWARTZ ROAD SOUTH OF SAN ANGELO. PATH WIDTH WAS APPROXIMATELY 80 YARDS WIDE.
A CAR WAS BLOWN OVER.
SEVERE DAMAGE TO THE BARRY BUILDING LOCATED AT HOUSTON HARTE AND GLENNA AVENUE IN SAN ANGELO. ALSO POWER LINES AND POLES DOWN. SEVERAL CARS FLIPPED OVER AND A TRACTOR TRAILER WAS OVERTURNED.

NWS STORM SURVEY FOUND TORNADO DAMAGE AT ARDEN ROAD AND GLENNA STREET ON THE WEST SIDE OF SAN ANGELO. A METAL ROOF WAS BLOWN OFF A BUILDING AND A TRACTOR TRAILER WAS OVERTURNED. THE DAMAGE PATH WAS 150 YARDS WIDE.
WIND GUSTS OF 60 TO 75 MPH WERE REPORTED.
NWS STORM SURVEY REVEALED AN EF0 TORNADO OVERTURNED A RV TRAVEL TRAILER AND CAUSED DAMAGE TO IRRIGATION EQUIPMENT OFF OF FORT MCKAVETT ROAD SOUTHWEST OF VICK.
A COUPLE OF POWER LINES AND TREES WERE KNOCKED DOWN BY THE DAMAGING THUNDERSTORM WINDS.
TREES AND ROAD SIGNS WERE BLOWN DOWN ON FM 503 ABOUT 8 MILES SOUTH OF DOOLE.
THERE WERE SEVERAL ROOFS DAMAGED WITH ONE ROOF BLOWN OFF.

A LAW ENFORCEMENT OFFICIAL WITH THE CITY OF BROWNWOOD REPORTED SEEING A TORNADO THAT WAS ILLUMINATED BY LIGHTNING. THE TORNADO WAS NARROW IN SIZE AS IT MOVED RAPIDLY THROUGH THE FIELD LOCATED JUST NORTH OF THE OFFICIALS LOCATION.
ROOFS WERE BLOWN OFF HOUSES AND A TRAILER WAS TURNED OVER. ALSO...TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN.
POWER LINES WERE BLOWN DOWN.
QUARTER SIZE HAIL WAS REPORTED IN BROWNWOOD.
PICKUP TRUCK WAS BLOWN OVER.
ROOF DAMAGE WAS NOTED IN TOWN.
HIGH WINDS WERE BREAKING SEVERAL 5 INCH LIMBS ON TREES.

NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE STORM SURVEY REVEALED THAT AN EF0 TORNADO CAUSED ROOF DAMAGE TO A HOME 5 MILES WEST-SOUTHWEST OF AVOCA. THERE WAS ALSO WIDESPREAD DAMAGE TO TREE LIMBS AND MANY UTILITY POLES SNAPPED ALONG U.S. 277.
ABOUT 20 POWER POLES WERE REPORTED DOWNED.
ROOF BLOWN OFF OF A BUILDING.
SEVERAL BUILDINGS WITH MODERATE DAMAGE.

NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE STORM SURVEY REVEALED THAT AN EF0 TORNADO CAUSED ROOF DAMAGE TO A HOME 5 MILES WEST-SOUTHWEST OF AVOCA. THERE WAS ALSO WIDESPREAD DAMAGE TO TREE LIMBS AND MANY UTILITY POLES SNAPPED ALONG U.S. 277.
QUARTER TO GOLFBALL SIZE HAIL REPORTED.
HAIL LOCATED 4 MILES NORTH OF GRAPE CREEK ON US-87.
HAIL IS COVERING GROUND.
HAIL LOCATATION ON THE WESTERN CONCHO COUNTY LINE ON US 87.
HAIL FALLING ON US 87 NEAR THE WESTERN CONCHO COUNTY BOUNDARY.
HAIL BROKE WINDSHIELD.
SEVERAL REPORTS IN THE CITY.
POWER LINES AND TREE LIMBS DOWNED.
QUARTER SIZE HAIL FELL AT THE SAN ANGELO AIRPORT.
TORNADO REPORTED APPROXIMATELY 4 MILES SOUTH OF LAKE SWEETWATER.
TORNADO WAS REPORTED BY THE PUBLIC ABOUT A QUARTER MILE EAST OF MELVIN.
DAMAGE TO TREES NEAR BRADY LAKE.
QUARTER SIZE HAIL REPORTED ALONG U.S. HIGHWAY 277 NEAR VIEW.
WIND GUST OF 60 KTS RECORDED AT BRADY AIRPORT AWOS BEFORE POWER WENT OUT.
SEVERAL POWER POLES WERE REPORTED SNAPPED NEAR BUFFALO GAP. A ROOF WAS ALSO BLOWN OFF A HOME AND TREES UPROOTED.
QUARTER SIZE HAIL COVERING U.S. HIGHWAY 283 2 MILES EAST OF LOHN.
A STORAGE BUILDING WAS BLOWN ACROSS THE STREET.
REPORT OF SMALL TREE LIMBS DOWN IN BRADY.
REPORT OF 3.5 INCH HAIL IN POTOSI.
GOLFBALL SIZE HAIL, 60 MPH WINDS, AND MINOR ROOF DAMAGE REPORTED.
ESTIMATE OF 70 MPH WIND GUSTS 10 MILES NORTHWEST OF CROSS PLAINS.
CR 2035 AND BITTER CREEK ROAD NEAR LAKE SWEETWATER WERE IMPASSIBLE DUE TO HIGH WATER.
POWER POLLS WERE BLOWN DOWN.
TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN AND SOME DAMAGE TO A GYM. HIGHWAY 70 WAS BLOCKED BY DEBRIS.
4 SEMI TRUCKS WERE BLOWN OVER BETWEEN 905-920 PM...1 WAS LOCATED 10 MILES EAST OF SWEETWATER, 1 IN SWEETWATER ON I-20 AND 2 WERE LOCATED ON NOLAN/FISHER COUNTY LINE ON I-20 AT 905 PM
FISHER COUNTY EMS REPORTS TREE LIMBS 2 INCHES IN DIAMETER SNAPPED AND IN THE ROAD ALONG SH70. TIME ESTIMATED.
POWER POLES WERE DOWNED AND SOME ROOF DAMAGE OCCURRED IN TOWN.
WIND MEASURED AT SWEETWATER AVENGER AIRPORT.
HIGHWAY 92 HAD WATER OVER THE ROAD.
HIGHWAY 1636 HAD WATER OVER THE ROAD.
HIGHWAYS 277 HAD WATER OVER THE ROAD.
HIGHWAY 2834 HAD OVER A FOOT OF WATER OVER THE ROAD.
PEAK WIND MEASURED AT ABILENE MUNICIPAL AIRPORT.
MEASURED IN KDYX OBSERVATION.
POWER POLES DOWNED IN TOWN.
LOW WATER CROSSINGS REPORTED FLOODED AND IMPASSABLE IN SOUTHERN CROCKETT COUNTY. RAINFALL OBSERVER REPORTED OVER 4 INCHES OF RAIN.
SOME STREET FLOODING IS OCCURRING IN ABILENE NEAR SOUTH 27TH STREET AND TREADWAY...AND ON CLACK STREET.
